rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are provided
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes unless they reference implementation-specific features
